zotero.abstractNote: The identification of nanoscale ordered structures in alloys is a challenging task in materials science, particularly when using atom probe tomography (APT) data. Here, we present a convolutional neural network (CNN)-based approach to automatically identify L12 ordered structures in face-centred cubic (fcc) alloys. We demonstrate that the CNN can accurately classify local atomic environments and distinguish between L12-ordered and disordered fcc structures. The method is robust against noise and missing atoms, which are common in experimental APT data. We apply our approach to a Co-Al-W-based superalloy and successfully map the spatial distribution of L12 precipitates. Our results show that the CNN-based method is significantly faster and more objective than conventional manual analysis, providing a powerful tool for the high-throughput characterization of complex alloy systems.
